title: kernels
summary:
Load compute kernels from the Hub 
 The Kernel Hub allows Python libraries and applications to load compute kernels directly from the Hubhttpshf.co. To support this kind of dynamic loading, Hub kernels differ from traditional Python kernel packages in that they are made to be same Python process. the different PyTorch build configurations various CUDA versions and C ABIs. Furthermore, older C library versions must be supported.

summary:
Code for https://jangirrishabh.github.io/lookcloser/ 
 Official PyTorch implementation for the paper Look Closer Bridging Egocentric and Third-Person Views with Transformers for Robotic Manipulation GPU access with CUDA 11.1 support is required. Install MuJoCo if you do not have it installed already Then, the remainder of the dependencies can be installed with the following commands

title: cuda-python
summary:
CUDA Python Low-level Bindings 
 CUDA Python is the home for accessing NVIDIAs CUDA platform from Python. It consists of multiple components CUDA Python is currently undergoing an overhaul to improve existing and bring up new components. All of the previously available functionalities from the cuda-python package will continue to be available, please refer to the cuda.bindingshttpsnvidia.github.iocuda-pythoncuda-bindingslatest documentation for installation guide and further detail.

title: pytorch_misc
summary:
Code snippets created for the PyTorch discussion board 
 Collection of code snippets I've written for the PyTorch discussion boardhttpsdiscuss.pytorch.org. All scripts were testes using the PyTorch 1.0 preview and torchvision 0.2.1. Additional libraries, e.g. numpy or pandas, are used in a few scripts. Some scripts might be a good starter to create a tutorial. Feedback is very welcome!

title: gsplat-kernel
summary: 2D Gaussian Splatting kernels for PyTorch, built with the HuggingFace kernel-builder system. python import torch import gsplat numgaussians 1000 means2d torch.randnumgaussians, 2, device'cuda' 0,1 coordinates scales2d torch.randnumgaussians, 2, device'cuda' 0.1 0.01 rotation torch.randnumgaussians, device'cuda' 2 3.14159 colors torch.randnumgaussians, 3, device'cuda' RGB

c/ua is the Docker Container for Computer-Use AI Agents.

title: light-the-torch
summary:
Install PyTorch distributions with computation backend auto-detection 
 light-the-torch is a small utility that wraps pip to ease the installation process for PyTorch distributions like torch, torchvision, torchaudio, and so on as well as third-party packages that depend on them. It auto-detects compatible CUDA versions from the local setup and installs the correct PyTorch binaries without user interference.

title: onnx-ir
summary: An in-memory IR that supports the full ONNX spec, designed for graph construction, analysis and transformation. Via pip pip install onnx-ir Or from source pip install githttpsgithub.comonnxir-py.git

title: ir-py
summary:
Efficient in-memory representation for ONNX, in Python 
 An in-memory IR that supports the full ONNX spec, designed for graph construction, analysis and transformation. Via pip pip install onnx-ir Or from source pip install githttpsgithub.comonnxir-py.git
